verifyNote: Plain-language clarifications, not legal advice. Confirm anything that affects you with an immigration attorney.

myths.myth: I can only take a W-2 job.
myths.reality: While your H-4 EAD is valid, it is unrestricted as to employer and work type — W-2 employment, 1099/freelance, consulting, sole proprietorship, or an LLC are all allowed. The EAD itself doesn't limit you to one form of work.
myths.caveat: True only WHILE the EAD is valid. Self-employment income has its own tax rules (Schedule C, self-employment tax).

myths.myth: I need a master's or a US degree to work.
myths.reality: H-4 EAD work is not tied to a specialty occupation or a prevailing-wage rule the way H-1B is. There is no degree requirement to work on a valid H-4 EAD.
myths.caveat: Specific jobs or licenses (e.g. nursing, real estate) may have their own credential requirements — that's separate from immigration.

myths.myth: If I work, it will hurt my spouse's H-1B or our green card.
myths.reality: Lawful work on a valid EAD does not by itself harm the H-1B or the green-card case. The real dependency runs the other way: your H-4 status — and therefore your EAD — depends on the H-1B spouse keeping valid H-1B status.
myths.caveat: If the H-1B lapses (e.g. job loss without a timely transfer), the H-4 and the EAD are affected, along with any job or business you run on it. Plan for that risk.

myths.myth: I'm not allowed to start an LLC.
myths.reality: You can form and own an LLC and actively work in your own business on a valid H-4 EAD. Anyone can passively own a US company; the EAD is what lets you actively work in it.
myths.caveat: Once the EAD expires (and with no auto-extension for renewals filed on/after Oct 30, 2025), you must stop actively working in the business until a new EAD is approved.

myths.myth: Once I have an EAD I'm set — it's 100% legal forever.
myths.reality: An H-4 EAD is real work authorization, but it is time-limited and must be renewed, and its validity is tied to maintaining H-4/H-1B status.
myths.caveat: Renewals filed on/after Oct 30, 2025 no longer get an automatic extension — see the renewal calculator and plan ahead to avoid a work gap.
